cases.setup: first requested name resolves to a throwing property; later requested name and same-name field are readable
cases.expected: fixture must record the current early-null behavior as adverse; approved replacement must continue deterministically or reject the member map

cases.setup: candidate exists only as a non-public member absent from the exact-profile allowlist
cases.expected: reject without invoking getter or field read

cases.setup: reference item has writable-looking quantity property whose setter throws
cases.expected: original quantity and owner graph remain byte-for-byte equivalent

cases.setup: value-type item member write succeeds locally; owner field write-back throws
cases.expected: owner and item state remain unchanged; no partial mutation

cases.setup: byte,sbyte,short,ushort,int,uint,long,ulong,float,double,decimal plus NaN, infinity and overflow inputs
cases.expected: only exact-profile allowlisted integral quantity types are accepted; fractional, NaN, infinity and unsupported types fail closed

cases.setup: currency text contains Wealth as a substring but exact currency identity is not Wealth
prefix mutation succeeds and original story Execute throws or is skipped
cases.expected: step amount is restored before failure escapes or finalizer completes

cases.setup: exact TG.Main metadata contains zero, one or multiple TradeUtils.Price overloads
cases.expected: accept exactly one fingerprint-bound overload with recorded seller,buyer,item,count positions; otherwise reject

cases.setup: seller or buyer is an exact-profile Hero subtype/proxy rather than exact FullName equality
cases.expected: direction derives from an allowlisted type relationship or fails closed; no string-name inference

cases.setup: overlapping patterns, wildcard, malformed Unicode and repeated hot-path evaluation
cases.expected: canonical identity, explicit precedence, one parse per config revision, bounded allocation and deterministic match

decision.reason: missing exact assembly fingerprints plus adverse atomicity, rollback, identity and hot-path behavior
